title: Loading Polish & PrepSection Accessibility
description: Replace StatsBar animate-pulse skeleton with shimmer, fix PrepSection accessibility (semantic section labels, aria-hidden on decorative bullet and plus icon, aria-controls on toggle button), add aria-label to KanbanBoard skeleton, add sr-only text for sort direction arrows in ApplicationListHeader, and add aria-labels to NPS score buttons in FeedbackWidget.

userStories.title: Replace StatsBar animate-pulse skeleton with shimmer
userStories.description: StatsBar.jsx MetricCard loading state uses animate-pulse rounded bg-muted classes instead of the project-standard shimmer-bg animate-shimmer. Replace the pulse skeleton with shimmer classes to match the loading pattern used throughout the rest of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsBar.jsx MetricCard loading skeleton uses shimmer-bg animate-shimmer instead of animate-pulse bg-muted
The skeleton shape (rounded rectangle placeholder) is preserved
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Replace PrepSection checklist label span with semantic heading
userStories.description: PrepSection.jsx renders section titles like 'Checklist' and 'Practice Questions' as plain <span> elements with no semantic role. These function as section headings. Replace them with <h3> (or appropriate heading level) so screen reader users can navigate by headings.
acceptanceCriteria: PrepSection.jsx section title elements ('Checklist', 'Practice Questions') use a semantic heading element (h3 or h4) instead of <span>
The visual styling is preserved via className
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to decorative bullet in PrepSection QuestionItem
userStories.description: PrepSection.jsx QuestionItem renders a bullet character '•' as visible decoration before each question. The bullet is purely decorative and should not be read by screen readers. Add aria-hidden='true' to the bullet element.
acceptanceCriteria: PrepSection.jsx QuestionItem bullet ('•') has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to decorative Plus icon in PrepSection AddQuestionItem
userStories.description: PrepSection.jsx AddQuestionItem renders a Plus icon that is purely decorative (the button's text already conveys the action). Add aria-hidden='true' to the Plus icon to prevent duplicate announcement.
acceptanceCriteria: PrepSection.jsx AddQuestionItem Plus icon has aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-controls to PrepSection section toggle button
userStories.description: PrepSection.jsx has a toggle button that expands/collapses a section, but the button is missing aria-controls pointing to the collapsible panel's id. Add an id to the collapsible panel and aria-controls={id} to the toggle button.
acceptanceCriteria: PrepSection.jsx toggle button has aria-controls pointing to the id of the collapsible panel
The collapsible panel has a matching id attribute
The toggle button already has aria-expanded — both aria-expanded and aria-controls should be present
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-label to KanbanBoard loading skeleton
userStories.description: KanbanBoard.jsx KanbanLoadingSkeleton renders with aria-busy='true' but no aria-label. Screen readers announce it without context. Add aria-label='Loading board' (or similar) to the skeleton container.
acceptanceCriteria: KanbanBoard.jsx KanbanLoadingSkeleton container has aria-label='Loading board' (or equivalent)
The container already has aria-busy='true' — both attributes should be present
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sr-only text for sort direction arrows in ApplicationListHeader
userStories.description: ApplicationListHeader.jsx renders sort direction arrows (↑/↓) as plain text characters with no screen reader context. A user navigating by keyboard cannot tell which column is sorted or in what direction. Wrap each arrow in a <span aria-hidden='true'> and add a <span className='sr-only'> sibling with the sort direction text (e.g. 'sorted ascending').
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationListHeader.jsx sort direction arrows (↑/↓) are wrapped in <span aria-hidden='true'>
A <span className='sr-only'> with the direction text ('sorted ascending' or 'sorted descending') is rendered adjacent to the arrow
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-labels to FeedbackWidget NPS score buttons
userStories.description: FeedbackWidget.jsx renders NPS score buttons (0–10) showing only the numeric value with no accessible name. Screen reader users hear just a number with no context. Add aria-label={`Score ${score}`} (or equivalent) to each score button.
acceptanceCriteria: FeedbackWidget.jsx NPS score buttons each have aria-label='Score N' where N is the numeric score
All 11 buttons (0 through 10) have unique aria-labels
Build passes with no errors
